generatedSentence: In a sprawling, retro-futuristic workplace, the daily payroll system perpetuated underpay, leaving many citizens desperately lacking. Young Kael harbored silent wishes for something beyond their shabby reality, often fiddling with a shiny screwdriver from his father's knickknack collection. His idiographic portfolios, filled with diagrams of unusual jackscrews and ancient mahjongg pieces, were noteworthy to him, yet everywhere he looked, a collective ignorance of the city’s entropy reigned. A newborn, unrealistic idea, a keystone for change, began to form in his mind, feeling samey amidst the unyielding routine. The wealthy keepers of the city maintained a backwards, lockstep reign, their off-take controlled by a shadowy warlock troupe. Kael objected internally, recognizing the faulty valve that kept society underway through rampant jobbery. He felt a lingering, icky premonition, like a horoscope predicting an unhinging event, as he visualized the black pyramids of power, their truth hidden behind a veil. The system, a complex jigsaw, mocked any intents for liberation, especially when he gladly opened a faulty valve of off-take and found it lacking. His resolve solidified after a tense encounter; the guards, moving with a gawky, lockstep rhythm, had mocked his subtle questioning. Kael, now panicky but determined, saw the city’s flyways as escape routes for the privileged, a constant reminder of the guardrail separating the classes. He knew his mission was underway. The reigning force, a lineage of vampish overlords, protected their payroll with enforcers known as 'linebackers' who were ready for a knockout. His tactical ingenuity involved a precise injector system he had painstakingly developed, designed to unhinge the oppressive control. In the ensuing confrontation, Kael, unarmed but quick, expertly deployed his device. The system, built on outdated rotations and historical data exhumes, began to fail, its checks unchecked. He imagined the daily nightlife of the corrupt, now facing an unexpected blackjack scenario. Though the battle felt like a hacky, illogical climax, it offered a glimpse of liberation, a ninth chance. With the old order’s opaque veil pulled back, a true proxy for justice could begin to take hold, ensuring the corrupt would underpay their dues, rather than collect their ill-gotten paychecks. The future, finally open and unlocked, promised a glimmer of hope.

Young Kael harbored silent wishes for something beyond their shabby reality, often fiddling with a shiny screwdriver from his father's knickknack collection. His idiographic portfolios, filled with diagrams of unusual jackscrews and ancient mahjongg pieces, were noteworthy to him, yet everywhere he looked, a collective ignorance of the city’s entropy reigned. A newborn, unrealistic idea, a keystone for change, began to form in his mind, feeling samey amidst the unyielding routine.

The wealthy keepers of the city maintained a backwards, lockstep reign, their off-take controlled by a shadowy warlock troupe. Kael objected internally, recognizing the faulty valve that kept society underway through rampant jobbery. He felt a lingering, icky premonition, like a horoscope predicting an unhinging event, as he visualized the black pyramids of power, their truth hidden behind a veil. The system, a complex jigsaw, mocked any intents for liberation, especially when he gladly opened a faulty valve of off-take and found it lacking.

His resolve solidified after a tense encounter; the guards, moving with a gawky, lockstep rhythm, had mocked his subtle questioning. Kael, now panicky but determined, saw the city’s flyways as escape routes for the privileged, a constant reminder of the guardrail separating the classes. He knew his mission was underway. The reigning force, a lineage of vampish overlords, protected their payroll with enforcers known as 'linebackers' who were ready for a knockout. His tactical ingenuity involved a precise injector system he had painstakingly developed, designed to unhinge the oppressive control.

In the ensuing confrontation, Kael, unarmed but quick, expertly deployed his device. The system, built on outdated rotations and historical data exhumes, began to fail, its checks unchecked. He imagined the daily nightlife of the corrupt, now facing an unexpected blackjack scenario. Though the battle felt like a hacky, illogical climax, it offered a glimpse of liberation, a ninth chance. With the old order’s opaque veil pulled back, a true proxy for justice could begin to take hold, ensuring the corrupt would underpay their dues, rather than collect their ill-gotten paychecks. The future, finally open and unlocked, promised a glimmer of hope.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean figure with an intricate chrome cybernetic forearm and a sleek, almost invisible earphone, wearing a practical, dark jumpsuit with subtle glowing accents. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Kael, in a dimly lit, vast industrial workplace filled with retro-futuristic machinery, studies holographic blueprints with a focused expression, a lone desk lamp illuminating his face, wide shot from above. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A shadowy, menacing figure, a Warlock Keeper with glowing eyes, seated on a throne overlooking a dystopian city skyline, gesturing dismissively towards a group of downtrodden citizens, low angle, dramatic lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, sprinting through a chaotic neon-lit market street, evading hulking, armored guards who are in hot pursuit, dynamic mid-shot from behind. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, standing triumphant amidst sparking, failing futuristic machinery, as the city's power grid visibly flickers and a distant explosion lights the sky, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.

definitions.definition: To spread; to expand into an open or loose position.

definitions.definition: To start (a campaign).

definitions.definition: To begin conducting business.
definitions.example: The shop opens at 9:00.

definitions.definition: To begin a side's innings as one of the first two batsmen.
To bet before any other player has in a particular betting round in a game of poker.
definitions.example: After the first two players fold, Julie opens for $5.

definitions.definition: To reveal one's hand.
definitions.example: Jeff opens his hand revealing a straight flush.

definitions.definition: (of a file, document, etc.) To load into memory for viewing or editing.

The act of making someone unconscious, or at least unable to come back on their feet within a certain period of time; a TKO.

definitions.definition: The deactivation of anything.

definitions.definition: A very attractive person, especially a beautiful woman.
A partially punched opening meant for optional later removal.

definitions.definition: The deactivation of a particular gene.

definitions.definition: Amazing; gorgeous; beautiful.

The act of turning around a centre or an axis.

definitions.definition: A regular variation in a sequence, such as to even-out wear, or people taking turns in a task; a duty roster.
definitions.example: In rotation, each member of the group would be responsible for the beacon fire.

definitions.definition: An operation on a metric space that is a continuous isometry and fixes at least one point.

The step during takeoff when the pilot commands the vehicle to lift the nose wheel off the ground during the takeoff roll. (see also: V2)

definitions.definition: To dig out of the ground; to take out of a place of burial; to disinter.
definitions.example: The archeologist exhumed artifacts from the ground with a shovel.

definitions.definition: To uncover; to bring to light.
Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A measure of the disorder present in a system.
definitions.example: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.

definitions.definition: The capacity factor for thermal energy that is hidden with respect to temperature http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055.

The dispersal of energy; how much energy is spread out in a process, or how widely spread out it becomes, at a specific temperature. https//web.archive.org/web/20060702234316/http://www.entropysite.com/students_approach.html

definitions.example: I don't think you can infer that from the premise. It's a faulty argument.

definitions.definition: At fault, to blame; guilty.
The part of the arm between the wrist and the elbow.
A section of the weapon between the receiver and the muzzle, used to hold the firearm steady.
(sometimes figurative) To arm in preparation.
Of technology, a concept, etc, so far advanced as to appear to be from the future.
The colour/color perceived in the absence of light, but also when no light is reflected, but rather absorbed.
A black dye or pigment.
A pen, pencil, crayon, etc., made of black pigment.
(in the plural) Black cloth hung up at funerals.
(sometimes capitalised) A person of African, Aborigine, or Maori descent; a dark-skinned person.
Blackness, the condition of having dark skin.
The black ball.
The edge of home plate.
A type of firecracker that is really more dark brown in colour.
Blackcurrant syrup (in mixed drinks, e.g. snakebite and black, cider and black).
(in chess and similar games) The person playing with the black set of pieces.
definitions.example: At this point black makes a disastrous move.

definitions.definition: Something, or a part of a thing, which is black.
A stain; a spot.
A dark smut fungus, harmful to wheat.
Marijuana.
To make black; to blacken.
To apply blacking to (something).
To boycott, usually as part of an industrial dispute.
(of an object) Absorbing all light and reflecting none; dark and hueless.
(of a place, etc) Without light.
(sometimes capitalized) Of or relating to any of various ethnic groups having dark pigmentation of the skin.
Designated for use by those ethnic groups which have dark pigmentation of the skin.
definitions.example: black drinking fountain; black hospital

definitions.definition: (of a card) Of the spades or clubs suits. Compare red
definitions.example: I was dealt two red queens, and he got one of the black queens.

definitions.definition: Bad; evil; ill-omened.
Expressing menace, or discontent; threatening; sullen.
definitions.example: He shot her a black look.

definitions.definition: (of objects, markets, etc) Illegitimate, illegal or disgraced.
Overcrowded.
(of coffee or tea) Without any cream, milk or creamer.
definitions.example: Jim drinks his coffee black, but Ellen prefers it with creamer.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the playing pieces of a board game deemed to belong to the "black" set (in chess the set used by the player who moves second) (often regardless of the pieces' actual colour).
Said of a symbol or character that is solid, filled with color. Compare white.
Related to the Christian Democratic Union of Germany.
definitions.example: After the election, the parties united in a black-yellow alliance.

definitions.definition: Clandestine; relating to a political, military, or espionage operation or site, the existence or details of which is withheld from the general public.
definitions.example: 5 percent of the Defense Department funding will go to black projects.

definitions.definition: Occult; relating to something (such as mystical or magical knowledge) which is unknown to or kept secret from the general public.
Protestant, often with the implication of being militantly pro-British or anti-Catholic
Having one or more features (hair, fur, armour, clothes, bark, etc) that is dark (or black); in taxonomy, especially: dark in comparison to another species with the same base name.
Foul; dirty.
A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
A serving of this beverage.
The seeds of the plant used to make coffee, misnamed ‘beans’ due to their shape.
The powder made by roasting and grinding the seeds.
A tropical plant of the genus Coffea.
A pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
The end of a meal, when coffee is served.
definitions.example: He did not stay for coffee.

definitions.definition: To drink coffee.
The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.
The process of striving to achieve equal rights and status.
The person or thing in the ninth position.
One of nine equal parts of a whole.

The compound interval between any tone and the tone represented on the ninth degree of the staff above it, as between one of the scale and two of the octave above; the octave of the second, consisting of 13 or 14 semitones (called minor and major ninth).
To lose a ninth
To divide by nine
The ordinal form of the number nine.
Unpleasantly sticky; yucky; disgusting.
definitions.example: I stepped in something icky and it smells terrible.

definitions.definition: Excessively sentimental.
Unwell or upset; in a bad state of mind or health.
definitions.example: I'm not going to work today. I just feel icky.

definitions.definition: A dish or foodstuff where two or more slices of bread serve as the wrapper or container of some other food.
(by extension) Any combination formed by layering one type of material between two layers of some other material.
A layer cake or sandwich cake.
To place one item between two other, usually flat, items
To put or set something between two others, in time.
Of a meal or serving size that is smaller than a dinner.
The same as something else, without variety, monotonous.
By mouth.
definitions.example: This medicine is taken orally. Swallowing a pill sure beats getting a shot every day.

definitions.definition: Spoken as opposed to written.
definitions.example: I took the make-up test orally because my arm is still in a cast.

definitions.definition: The top stone of an arch.
Something on which other things depend for support.
A native or resident of the American state of Pennsylvania.
(retail) A retail price that is double the cost price; a markup of 100%.
The combination of the shortstop and second baseman.
To distort (an image) by projecting it onto a surface at an angle, which for example causes a square to look like a trapezoid.
(retail) To double the cost price in order to determine the retail price; to apply a markup of 100%.
The direction opposed to the pull of gravity.
definitions.example: Up is a good way to go.

definitions.definition: A positive thing.
definitions.example: I hate almost everything about my job. The only up is that it's so close to home.

definitions.definition: An upstairs room of a two story house.
definitions.example: She lives in a two-up two-down.

definitions.definition: To increase or raise.
definitions.example: If we up the volume, we may be able to hear what he says.

definitions.definition: To promote.
definitions.example: It wasn’t long before they upped him to Vice President.

definitions.definition: (usually in combination with another verb) To act suddenly.
definitions.example: He just upped and quit.

definitions.definition: To ascend; to climb up.
To upload.
definitions.example: 100 new apps and games have just been upped.

definitions.definition: Facing upwards.
definitions.example: Put the notebook face up on the table.

definitions.definition: On or at a physically higher level.
definitions.example: The flood waters are up again across large areas of the country.

definitions.definition: Headed, or designated to go, upward, as an escalator, stairway, elevator etc.
definitions.example: Where is the up escalator?

definitions.definition: Fitted or fixed at a high or relatively high position, especially on a wall or ceiling.
definitions.example: The Christmas decorations are up.

definitions.definition: Aloft.
definitions.example: The kite is up!

definitions.definition: Raised; lifted.
definitions.example: Don't go into the living room just now – I've got the carpet up.

definitions.definition: Built, constructed.
definitions.example: Are the new buildings up yet?

definitions.definition: Standing.
definitions.example: The audience were up and on their feet.

definitions.definition: Awake and out of bed.
definitions.example: I can’t believe it’s 3 a.m. and you’re still up.

definitions.definition: Riding the horse; mounted.
(of the sun or moon) Above the horizon, in the sky.
definitions.example: It'll get warmer once the sun's up.

definitions.definition: Larger; greater in quantity, volume, value etc.
definitions.example: My temperature is up this morning.

definitions.definition: Indicating a larger or higher quantity.
definitions.example: The barometer is up, so fine weather should be on the way.

definitions.definition: Ahead; leading; winning.
definitions.example: The home team were up by two goals at half-time.

definitions.definition: Available; made public; posted.
definitions.example: Is your new video up yet? I looked on the website, but I couldn't find it.

definitions.definition: Finished, to an end
definitions.example: Her contract is up next month, so it's time to negotiate another one.

definitions.definition: In a good mood.
definitions.example: I’m feeling up today.

definitions.definition: (usually in the phrase up for) Willing; ready.
definitions.example: If you are up for a trip, let’s go.

definitions.definition: Next in a sequence.
definitions.example: Smith is up to bat.

definitions.definition: (not used attributively) Happening; new; of concern. See also what's up with.
definitions.example: What is up with that project at headquarters?

definitions.definition: (postnominal) Said of the higher-ranking pair in a two pair.
definitions.example: AAKK = aces up

definitions.definition: Well-informed; current.
definitions.example: I’m not up on the latest news. What’s going on?

definitions.definition: Functional; working.
definitions.example: Is the server back up?

definitions.definition: (of a railway line or train) Traveling towards a major terminus.
definitions.example: The London train is on the up line.

definitions.definition: (bartending) Chilled and served without ice.
definitions.example: A Cosmopolitan is typically served up.

definitions.definition: Erect.
(graffiti) well-known; renowned
Away from the surface of the Earth or other planet; in opposite direction to the downward pull of gravity.
definitions.example: I looked up and saw the airplane overhead.

definitions.definition: To or at a physically higher or more elevated position.
definitions.example: All day we climbed up and up.

definitions.definition: To a higher level of some quantity or notional quantity, such as price, volume, pitch, happiness, etc.
definitions.example: Cheer up, the weekend's almost here.

definitions.definition: To or in a position of equal advance or equality; not short of, back of, less advanced than, away from, etc.; usually followed by to or with.
definitions.example: A stranger came up and asked me for directions.

definitions.definition: (intensifier) Used as an aspect marker to indicate a completed action or state; thoroughly, completely.
definitions.example: Can you sum up your research?

definitions.definition: To or from one's possession or consideration.
definitions.example: I picked up some milk on the way home.

definitions.definition: North.
definitions.example: I live in Florida, but I'm going up to New York to visit my family this weekend.

definitions.definition: Towards or at a central place, or any place that is visualised as 'up' by virtue of local features or local convention, or arbitrarily, irrespective of direction or elevation change.
definitions.example: He lives up by the railway station.

definitions.definition: Towards the principal terminus, towards milepost zero.
Aside, so as not to be in use.
definitions.example: to lay up riches; put up your weapons

definitions.definition: Against the wind or current.
(Cartesian graph) In a positive vertical direction.
Relatively close to the batsman.
definitions.example: The bowler pitched the ball up.

definitions.definition: (bartending) Without additional ice.
definitions.example: Would you like that drink up or on ice?

definitions.definition: (academia) To university, especially to Cambridge or Oxford.
definitions.example: She's going up to read Classics this September.

definitions.definition: Toward the top of.
definitions.example: The cat climbed up the tree.

definitions.definition: Toward the center, source, or main point of reference; toward the end at which something is attached.
definitions.example: I felt something crawling up my arm.

definitions.definition: From south to north of.
definitions.example: We sailed up the East Coast of England from Ipswich to South Shields.

definitions.definition: Further along (in any direction).
definitions.example: Go up the street until you see the sign.

definitions.definition: From the mouth towards the source of (a river or waterway).
definitions.example: He led an expedition up the Amazon.

definitions.definition: Of a man: having sex with.
definitions.example: Phwoar, look at that bird. I'd love to be up her.

definitions.definition: At (a given place, especially one imagined to be higher or more distant from a central location).
definitions.example: I'll see you later up the snooker club.

definitions.definition: The position of the planets and stars at the moment of someone's birth; a diagram of such positions.
An astrological forecast of a person's future based on such information.
To envisage, or form a mental picture (of something).
To make (something) visible.
Any condition, circumstance, opportunity or means, particularly favorable to success, or to any desired end.
definitions.example: The enemy had the advantage of a more elevated position.

definitions.definition: Superiority; mastery; — used with of to specify its nature or with over to specify the other party.
Superiority of state, or that which gives it; benefit; gain; profit
definitions.example: Having the faster car is of little advantage.

definitions.definition: The score where one player wins a point after deuce but needs the next to carry the game.
The continuation of the game after a foul against the attacking team, because the attacking team are in an advantageous position.
Interest of money; increase; overplus (as the thirteenth in the baker's dozen).
An advantage.
A place or position affording a good view; a vantage point.
A superior or more favorable situation or opportunity; gain; profit; advantage.
In a state of panic.
The improper or corrupt act or conduct of public or official business for the sake of private gain.
A jack (mechanical lifting device) which is operated by turning a leadscrew.
A transducer that converts electric signals into sound and is held near the ear, especially as part of a telephone; an earpiece or headphone.
The place where someone works.
One who teases or pokes fun.
(textile treatment) A person or thing that teases.
A preview or part of a product released in preparation of its main advertising, typically a short film, song or quote.
A kind of gull, the jaeger.
A shunt winding on field magnets for maintaining their magnetism when the main circuit is open.
The stoker of a glassworks furnace.
A short horizontal curtain used to mask the flies and frame the top of the inner stage opening, adjustable to the desired height.
The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to its posterior and near the anus.
definitions.example: Most primates have a tail and fangs.

definitions.definition: An object or part of an object resembling a tail in shape, such as the thongs on a cat-o'-nine-tails.
The back, last, lower, or inferior part of anything.
The feathers attached to the pygostyle of a bird.
The tail-end of an object, e.g. the rear of an aircraft's fuselage, containing the tailfin.
The rear structure of an aircraft, the empennage.
The visible stream of dust and gases blown from a comet by the solar wind.
The latter part of a time period or event, or (collectively) persons or objects represented in this part.
The part of a distribution most distant from the mode; as, a long tail.
One who surreptitiously follows another.
The lower order of batsmen in the batting order, usually specialist bowlers.
The lower loop of the letters in the Roman alphabet, as in g, q or y.
(chiefly in the plural) The side of a coin not bearing the head; normally the side on which the monetary value of the coin is indicated; the reverse.
All the last terms of a sequence, from some term on.
The buttocks or backside.
The penis of a person or animal.
Sexual intercourse.
definitions.example: I'm gonna get me some tail tonight.

definitions.definition: (kayaking) The stern; the back of the kayak.
A train or company of attendants; a retinue.
The distal tendon of a muscle.
A filamentous projection on the tornal section of each hind wing of certain butterflies.
A downy or feathery appendage of certain achens, formed of the permanent elongated style.
A portion of an incision, at its beginning or end, which does not go through the whole thickness of the skin, and is more painful than a complete incision; called also tailing.
One of the strips at the end of a bandage formed by splitting the bandage one or more times.
A rope spliced to the strap of a block, by which it may be lashed to anything.
The part of a note which runs perpendicularly upward or downward from the head; the stem.
A tailing.
The bottom or lower portion of a member or part such as a slate or tile.
A tailcoat.
To follow and observe surreptitiously.
definitions.example: Tail that car!

definitions.definition: To hold by the end; said of a timber when it rests upon a wall or other support; with in or into
To swing with the stern in a certain direction; said of a vessel at anchor.
definitions.example: This vessel tails downstream.

definitions.definition: To follow or hang to, like a tail; to be attached closely to, as that which can not be evaded.
To pull or draw by the tail.
A formal evening jacket with an extended back panel; a dress coat.
Any coat with similar tails.
The side of a coin that doesn't bear the picture of the head of state or similar.
definitions.example: Tails, I win.

definitions.definition: Tailings; waste
A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.
An inspection or examination.
definitions.example: I don't know if she will be there, but it's worth a check.

definitions.definition: A control; a limit or stop.
definitions.example: The castle moat should hold the enemy in check.

definitions.definition: A mark (especially a checkmark: ✓) used as an indicator.
definitions.example: Place a check by the things you have done.

definitions.definition: An order to a bank to pay money to a named person or entity.
definitions.example: I was not carrying cash, so I wrote a check for the amount.

definitions.definition: A bill, particularly in a restaurant.
definitions.example: I summoned the waiter, paid the check, and hurried to leave.

definitions.definition: A maneuver performed by a player to take another player out of the play.
definitions.example: The hockey player gave a good hard check to obtain the puck.

definitions.definition: A token used instead of cash in gaming machines, or in gambling generally.
A lengthwise separation through the growth rings in wood.
A mark, certificate or token by which errors may be prevented, or a thing or person may be identified.
definitions.example: a check given for baggage

definitions.definition: The forsaking by a hawk of its proper game to follow other birds.
A small chink or crack.
To inspect; to examine.
definitions.example: Check the oil in your car once a month.

definitions.definition: To verify the accuracy of a text or translation, usually making some corrections (proofread) or many (copyedit).

(often used with "off") To mark items on a list (with a checkmark or by crossing them out) that have been chosen for keeping or removal or that have been dealt with (for example, completed or verified as correct or satisfactory).
definitions.example: Check off the items that you've checked (inspected).

definitions.definition: To control, limit, or halt.
definitions.example: Check your enthusiasm during a negotiation.

definitions.definition: To verify or compare with a source of information.
definitions.example: Check your data against known values.

definitions.definition: To leave in safekeeping.
definitions.example: Check your hat and coat at the door.

definitions.definition: To leave with a shipping agent for shipping.
definitions.example: Check your bags at the ticket counter before the flight.

definitions.definition: To pass or bounce the ball to an opponent from behind the three-point line and have the opponent pass or bounce it back to start play.
definitions.example: He checked the ball and then proceeded to perform a perfect layup.

definitions.definition: To disrupt another player with the stick or body to obtain possession of the ball or puck.
definitions.example: The hockey player checked the defenceman to obtain the puck.

definitions.definition: To remain in a hand without betting. Only legal if no one has yet bet.
definitions.example: Tom didn't think he could win, so he checked.

definitions.definition: To make a move which puts an adversary's king in check; to put in check.
To chide, rebuke, or reprove.
To slack or ease off, as a brace which is too stiffly extended.
To crack or gape open, as wood in drying; or to crack in small checks, as varnish, paint, etc.
To make checks or chinks in; to cause to crack.
definitions.example: The sun checks timber.

definitions.definition: To make a stop; to pause; with at.
To clash or interfere.
To act as a curb or restraint.
To turn, when in pursuit of proper game, and fly after other birds.
(usually pluralized) A pattern made up of a grid of squares of alternating colors; a checkered pattern.
definitions.example: The tablecloth had red and white checks.

definitions.definition: Any fabric woven with such a pattern.
To mark with a check pattern.
To become fastened in place.
definitions.example: If you put the brakes on too hard, the wheels will lock.

definitions.definition: To fasten with a lock.
definitions.example: Remember to lock the door when you leave.

definitions.definition: To be capable of becoming fastened in place.
definitions.example: This door locks with a key.

definitions.definition: To intertwine or dovetail.
definitions.example: We locked arms and stepped out into the night.

definitions.definition: (break dancing) To freeze one's body or a part thereof in place.
definitions.example: a pop and lock routine

definitions.definition: To furnish (a canal) with locks.
To raise or lower (a boat) in a lock.
To seize (e.g. the sword arm of an antagonist) by turning the left arm around it, to disarm him.
To modify (a thread) so that users cannot make new posts in it.
(WMF jargon) To prevent a page from being edited by other users.
definitions.example: Frequently vandalized pages are generally locked to prevent further damage.

definitions.definition: Having undergone locking; secured by a lock.
definitions.example: A locked door barred the way.

definitions.definition: (Dublin) Very drunk.
(of a phone) Bound to a carrier.
A woman with an excessive libido.
definitions.example: She's so horny she must be a nymphomaniac.

definitions.definition: (of a woman) Having an excessive libido.
Nocturnal activities, especially visiting nightclubs.
definitions.example: Rio de Janeiro boasts the best nightlife in South America.

definitions.definition: Something that is produced, consumed, used, or done every day.
To drive an automobile frequently, on a daily basis, for regular and mundane tasks.
That occurs every day, or at least every working day
Diurnal, by daylight, as opposed to nightly
Any of various devices that are used to inject something.
An object that realizes a dependency injection.
Defenceless and lacking weapons or armour.
Not carrying arms.
Not having thorns or claws etc.
Unaided, as of the vision without a glass.
Unrestrained, not held back.
Not examined for accuracy, efficiency, etc.
Of a check box: not checked (ticked or enabled).
(crossword puzzles) Of a square: part of only one entry (that is, across or down, but not both).
Like a vamp (flirtatious woman).
A clear distilled alcoholic liquor made from grain mash.
A serving of the above beverage.
Neutral spirits distilled (or treated after distillation) so as to have no distinctive character, aroma, taste or color.
(baby-talk) Urine.
(baby-talk) The genitalia (as used for urination), especially the penis.
In a glad manner; happily.
Willingly; certainly.
definitions.example: Gladly, thank you.

definitions.definition: A (generally human) male who begets a child.
definitions.example: My father was a strong influence on me.

definitions.definition: A male ancestor more remote than a parent; a progenitor; especially, a first ancestor.
A term of respectful address for an elderly man.
definitions.example: Come, father; you can sit here.

definitions.definition: A term of respectful address for a priest.
A person who plays the role of a father in some way.
definitions.example: My brother was a father to me after my parents got divorced.

definitions.definition: The founder of a discipline or science.
definitions.example: Albert Einstein is the father of modern physics.

definitions.definition: Something that is the greatest or most significant of its kind.
Something inanimate that begets.
A senator of Ancient Rome.
To be a father to; to sire.
To give rise to.
To act as a father; to support and nurture.
To provide with a father.
To adopt as one's own.
A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
